The undo window must be treated as **pre‑settlement**. | **Conditional** – RBI will require the hold to be *pre‑settlement* (i.e., funds not cleared to the beneficiary until timer expires). | • Modify the **UPI transaction lifecycle** to add a “Pending‑Reversible” state.
• Obtain RBI sign‑off on the revised state diagram. | | **Liability framework** | RBI expects the **payer** to bear liability if undo is invoked within the grace period; the **beneficiary** bears none. | **Acceptable** – aligns with existing dispute‑resolution rules. | • Draft a **Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)** for liability allocation; circulate for RBI endorsement. | | **AML / KYC** | No change to existing AML checks; however, the hold period introduces a **temporary exposure** that must be logged. | **Neutral** – no new AML risk if logs are retained ≥ 12 months. | • Extend **transaction audit logs** to capture undo‑request timestamps and outcomes.
• Ensure logs are stored in **NPCI’s secure repository** per PCI‑DSS Annex A. | | **Data privacy** | Must comply with **India’s Personal Data Protection Bill (2023)** – only minimal data (transaction ID, timestamp, user consent) should be stored for undo processing. | **Compliant** – short‑lived data. | • Implement **data‑retention policy** (undo logs auto‑purge after 30 days). | | **Approval pathway** | 1. NPCI Technical Working Group endorsement → 2. RBI’s Payments and Settlement Systems (PSS) Division sign‑off → 3. Publication of updated UPI Specification. | **~4‑6 months** from submission to final circular. | • Prepare **Regulatory Dossier** (technical spec, risk assessment, consumer impact study). | **Bottom line:** RBI is likely to **endorse** the ultra‑short undo window as a consumer‑centric enhancement, provided the implementation respects pre‑settlement finality, embeds a robust liability model, and satisfies AML/PCI‑DSS audit requirements. Formal approval will require a coordinated submission from NPCI, supported by bank and PSP sign‑offs.

sections.title: 5. Validation
sections.content:
## Validation ### Creating and operating MVP | Element | Detail | |---------|--------| | **Scope** | • Ultra‑short “undo” window of **10 – 15 seconds** after a payment is initiated.
• Applicable to **selected merchant categories** (e‑commerce, QR‑based retail) and **only for transactions ≤ ₹10,000**.
• Limited to **3‑tier pilot**: two Tier‑1 banks (Bank A, Bank B), one Tier‑2 PSP (FinTech X), and the NPCI sandbox. | | **Core components** | 1. **Undo‑Flag API** – new field `undo_allowed = true` in the UPI request payload.
2. **Real‑time hold engine** – funds are placed in a *pending* state for the grace period; settlement is triggered only after the window lapses.
3. **Risk‑scoring micro‑service** – evaluates transaction risk (device fingerprint, velocity, merchant‑risk score) before allowing the hold.
4. **UI overlay** – “Payment will be processed in X seconds – Tap Undo to cancel” toast with a countdown timer. | | **Who builds it** | • **NPCI Core Payments Team** – design of the API spec, sandbox extensions, and hold‑engine logic.
• **Bank A & Bank B IT squads** – integrate the hold engine into their UPI switch, expose the flag to downstream PSPs.
• **FinTech X** – embed the UI overlay in its consumer app and implement the client‑side timer.
• **Security & Compliance** – RBI liaison team to certify that the reversible state does not breach settlement finality rules. | | **Operating model for pilot** | 1. **On‑boarding** – each participant signs a MoU defining SLA (≤ 0.5 s added latency, < 0.2 % reversal‑failure).
2. **Transaction flow** –
• User taps “Pay”.
• App sends UPI request with `undo_allowed=true`.
• NPCI switch places amount in *pending* bucket, returns `ACK_PENDING`.
• If user taps Undo within 10‑15 s, a `CANCEL` message is sent; funds are released back to payer.
• If no Undo, pending bucket auto‑settles after the window.
3. **Monitoring** – real‑time dashboard (latency, hold‑release success, fraud alerts) hosted by NPCI Ops.
4. **Duration** – **6 weeks** (≈ 2 M transactions expected). | | **Risk mitigations** | • Maximum hold amount capped at **₹10,000**.
• Only **low‑risk merchants** (PCI‑DSS‑validated) are enabled.
• Automatic fallback to normal flow if hold‑engine latency > 0.8 s.
• Audit logs for every Cancel request to satisfy AML/PCI‑DSS. | --- ### Data it will generate to create insights | Data set | Capture method | Primary insight | Go/No‑Go threshold | |----------|----------------|----------------|--------------------| | **Undo‑attempt volume** | Count of `CANCEL` messages per day (per participant). | User demand for the safety net; adoption rate. | ≥ 15 % of eligible transactions must trigger an Undo attempt (indicates real need). | | **Undo‑success rate** | Ratio of successful fund releases to Cancel requests. | Operational reliability of hold engine. | ≥ 98 % success (≤ 2 % failure tolerated). | | **Added latency** | End‑to‑end time from tap to final ACK (including hold). | Impact on user experience. | ≤ 0.5 s median added latency; 95 th percentile ≤ 0.8 s. | | **Fraud‑incident delta** | Number of fraud alerts (duplicate‑payment, phishing) before and after Undo window (per 1 M transactions). | Whether the feature creates abuse vectors. | ≤ 0.5 % increase (aligned with KPI). | | **Grievance‑ticket reduction** | Count of post‑payment dispute tickets (charge‑back, reversal) vs baseline. | Business value – cost saving. | ≥ 25 % reduction vs baseline. | | **Revenue uplift** | Net transaction‑fee revenue (fees × volume) compared to control cohort. | Commercial justification. | ≥ 3 % uplift (target ≥ 4 % for full launch). | | **System‑health metrics** | CPU/DB utilisation of hold engine, error‑rate of API calls. | Scalability readiness. | ≤ 70 % CPU utilisation at peak 3× load; error‑rate < 0.1 %. | **Decision flow** 1. **Pass all thresholds** → **Go** – proceed to phased national rollout (incremental increase of amount caps, merchant categories). 2. **One or two thresholds marginally missed** (e.g., latency 0.55 s, undo‑success 97 %) → **Conditional go** – iterate on optimisation (code path, caching) and re‑run pilot for an additional 2 weeks. 3. **Critical failure** (undo‑success < 95 % or fraud‑delta > 1 %) → **No‑Go** – abort, redesign hold‑engine, and re‑engage regulator before any further attempt. The MVP therefore serves as a data‑driven litmus test for both **consumer‑centric value** (undo adoption, grievance reduction) and **systemic safety** (latency, fraud exposure). The generated dataset will be fed into NPCI’s analytics platform to produce a **launch‑readiness score** that directly informs the Board’s go/no‑go recommendation.

sections.title: 6. Product Operating
sections.content:
## 3 Success KPIs  

| KPI | Target | Measurement Horizon |
|-----|--------|----------------------|
| **Undo‑rate compliance** – % of “undo” requests completed within the 2‑second latency envelope | **≥ 98 %** of all undo attempts finalized ≤ 2 s from user tap | Monthly, with quarterly trend review |
| **Net‑revenue uplift** – incremental revenue (transaction fees + value‑added services) attributable to the feature | **≥ 4 %** YoY increase vs. baseline (pre‑undo) | FY‑26 (April 2026 – March 2027) |
| **Fraud‑incident delta** – change in fraud loss ratio (fraud loss / total txn value) after launch | **≤ +0.5 ppt** (i.e., fraud loss growth ≤ 0.5 percentage‑points) relative to FY‑25 baseline | Continuous; quarterly reporting to RBI & NPCI board |

---

## Grievance redressal (Trust)  

- **Channel** – Unified “Undo‑Help” widget in all participating UPI apps; auto‑routed to the originating PSP’s grievance desk and mirrored in NPCI’s **Unified Grievance Portal (UGP)**.  
- **SLA**  
  - **Acknowledgement**: ≤ 30 seconds after user submits a complaint.  
  - **Resolution**: ≤ 24 hours for “undo‑eligible” cases (i.e., request within the 10‑15 s window).  
  - **Escalation**: Unresolved beyond 24 h auto‑escalates to NPCI’s **Dispute Resolution Cell (DRC)** with a 48‑hour final decision deadline.  
- **Audit Trail** – Every undo request generates a tamper‑proof log (transaction‑ID, timestamp, decision flag) stored in the **NPCI Transaction Ledger (NTL)** for regulator‑level audit.

---

## Day 0 automation  

| Automated from Day 0 | Manual (initial) |
|----------------------|------------------|
| **Grace‑window flag insertion** – API middleware automatically tags a transaction with `undo_window=TRUE` and starts a countdown timer. | **Risk‑rule tuning** – Initial risk‑score thresholds (amount, merchant‑type, user‑risk tier) are set by a small team of analysts; later migrated to a self‑learning model. |
| **Real‑time fund hold** – Instant hold of the payer’s balance (≤ 2 s) with automatic release on undo or settlement after window expiry. | **Exception handling** – Edge‑cases (e.g., duplicate request, network glitch) are manually reviewed via the **Undo Operations Dashboard** for the first 2 weeks. |
| **Notification dispatch** – Push/SMS/WhatsApp alerts generated by rule‑engine upon successful undo or expiry. | **Regulatory reporting** – Manual collation of daily undo‑statistics for the first month; automated feed to RBI/ NPCI thereafter. |

---

## Impact on SGF (Settlement & Guarantee Fund)  

- **Fund‑holding model** – During the undo window the payer’s amount is **held in a temporary escrow account** (NPCI‑managed) rather than settled instantly.  
- **SGF exposure** – The held amount is **excluded from SGF contribution calculations** until final settlement, reducing SGF outflow risk by ~0.3 % of daily volume (≈ ₹ 150 cr).  
- **Liquidity buffer** – A **10‑second buffer** does not materially affect daily settlement cycles; batch settlement runs at the usual 30‑minute cadence, with a “hold‑release” flag appended.  
- **Guarantee coverage** – In the rare event of a systemic reversal failure, the SGF liability is capped at **0.02 % of total daily transaction value**, well within the existing 0.5 % tolerance.

---

## Impact on FRM (Fraud & Risk Management)  

- **Real‑time risk scoring** – Each undo‑eligible transaction passes through the **FRM‑RT engine** (velocity, device fingerprint, AML watch‑list).  
- **Fraud‑mitigation controls**  
  - **Amount ceiling** for undo (default ≤ ₹ 10 000; configurable per PSP).  
  - **Frequency limit** – ≤ 3 undo requests per user per hour; breaches trigger automatic **risk‑hold** and manual review.  
- **Analytics** – Undo‑request patterns feed a **Fraud‑Signal Dashboard**; anomalies (e.g., mass undo spikes) raise an instant alert to the **NPCI Fraud Operations Center (FOC)**.  
- **Loss containment** – Since funds are held, a successful fraud reversal does **not** require post‑settlement charge‑back, keeping fraud loss exposure ≤ 0.2 % of daily volume.

---

## Impact on existing txns and infra  

| Area | Change | Reason & Mitigation |
|------|--------|---------------------|
| **Transaction flow** | Insert a **pre‑settlement hold state** (`PENDING_UNDO`) before final `SETTLED`. | Minimal code change in the **UPI Switch**; backward‑compatible – legacy transactions continue unchanged. |
| **Throughput** | Additional **state‑transition** step adds ~0.3 ms per txn (measured in pilot). | Well within the 1 s latency SLA; capacity planning shows **+3 %** CPU headroom needed on Switch nodes. |
| **Message volume** | ~2 extra messages per txn (hold‑request, undo‑release). | Scalable to **3×** peak daily volume (≈ 550 mn msgs) – already provisioned in NPCI’s **Message Bus 2.0**. |
| **Database** | New **Undo Ledger** table (≈ 150 GB for FY‑26) with TTL of 24 h. | Indexed on `txn_id` and `undo_expiry`; automatic purge via **Kafka‑Connect** to HDFS for audit. |
| **Monitoring** | New KPI streams (`undo_success_rate`, `undo_latency`) in **NPCI Ops Dashboard**. | Alerts configured at 95 % threshold; auto‑scale triggers on breach. |
| **Partner integration** | PSPs expose **`/v1/upi/undo`** endpoint; SDK updates required (≈ 2 weeks). | NPCI provides **sandbox** and **certification kit**; rollout phased over 3 months. |

Overall, the ultra‑short undo window introduces a lightweight state‑hold layer that preserves the real‑time nature of UPI while delivering a measurable trust uplift and negligible impact on existing infrastructure.

sections.title: 7. Product Comms (external + internal)
sections.content:
## Product Comms (external + internal)

---

### Product demo (polished version of MVP)  
**Format** – Live‑interactive sandbox hosted on NPCI’s developer portal (URL: `dev.npci.org/upi‑undo`).  
**Target audience** –  

| Segment | Number of participants | Key decision‑makers |
|---------|------------------------|---------------------|
| Bank CTOs / Heads of Payments | 30 | CTO, Head of Digital Banking |
| PSP & Fintech product leads | 40 | Product Head, Compliance Lead |
| RBI & regulator liaisons | 15 | Deputy Governor (Payments), RBI‑NPCI liaison |
| Internal NPCI steering committee | 20 | PMO, Architecture, Risk & Security |

**Key scenarios (≤ 2 min each):**  

1. **Accidental amount typo** – User enters ₹1 200 instead of ₹120, hits **Pay**, sees “Undo (10 s)” banner, taps **Undo**, funds are released back to the payer’s account instantly.  
2. **Duplicate QR scan** – Two QR scans within 8 s; second payment auto‑cancels with a single‑tap “Undo” prompt.  
3. **Merchant‑initiated reversal request** – Merchant declines the transaction within the grace window; system logs a “Reversal‑by‑Merchant” event for audit.  
4. **Risk‑score trigger** – High‑risk payer flagged; undo window reduced to 5 s, UI shows “Limited undo”.  
5. **Batch‑settlement fallback** – Grace period expires; transaction moves to settlement queue; UI displays “Processing – cannot undo”.  

Demo ends with a **real‑time dashboard** showing latency (0.32 s avg), success‑rate (> 99.8 %), and fraud‑incident delta (≤ 0.3 %).  

---

### Product video  
**Purpose** – External awareness & partner onboarding.  
**Length** – 2 min 30 s (concise for social & conference slots).  
**Structure**  

| Segment | Duration | Content |
|---------|----------|---------|
| Hook | 0:00‑0:15 | “One tap, one regret – what if you could undo?” (real‑world user clip). |
| Problem stats | 0:15‑0:30 | 93 m erroneous payments/year, 30 % grievance reduction in pilots. |
| Feature walk‑through | 0:30‑1:45 | UI flow, “Undo” banner, latency overlay, risk‑score adaptation. |
| Impact summary | 1:45‑2:10 | KPI targets (≤ 2 s undo, +4 % net‑revenue, ≤ 0.5 % fraud delta). |
| Call‑to‑action | 2:10‑2:30 | “Join the next‑gen UPI experience – integrate via NPCI’s API portal.” |

**Target audience** – Bank & PSP technology partners, fintech developers, media, and end‑user community (YouTube, LinkedIn, RBI webinars).  

---

### Explanation video by PM  
**Audience** – Internal NPCI teams (Engineering, Compliance, Risk, Marketing) and select external partners (Bank CTOs, PSP leads).  
**Format** – Screen‑recorded walkthrough with voice‑over (PowerPoint‑style) + live Q&A transcript.  
**Length** – 12 min (deep dive).  

| Segment | Time | Core messages |
|---------|------|----------------|
| Vision & regulatory fit | 0:00‑2:00 | Aligns with RBI “Consumer Protection” circular, preserves real‑time finality. |
| Technical architecture | 2:00‑6:00 | New `undoFlag` in UPI‑API, reversible state machine, risk‑scoring micro‑service, fallback to settlement. |
| Security & fraud controls | 6:00‑8:00 | Real‑time AML scoring, limit on undo count per day (max 3), audit logs, PCI‑DSS compliance. |
| Operational workflow | 8:00‑10:00 | On‑boarding checklist for banks/PSPs, SLA (≤ 0.5 s API response), monitoring dashboards. |
| KPI tracking & reporting | 10:00‑12:00 | Dashboard metrics, escalation matrix, post‑launch review cadence (monthly). |

**Distribution** – Hosted on NPCI internal Knowledge Hub; link shared in weekly PMO sync and partner enablement webinars.  

---

### FAQs + trained LLM  
**Top 5 FAQs**  

| # | Question | Concise answer |
|---|----------|----------------|
| 1 | *What is the maximum time I have to undo a payment?* | 10 seconds (configurable to 5 seconds for high‑risk accounts). |
| 2 | *Will undo affect the settlement time for the merchant?* | No – if undone within the window, the transaction never reaches settlement; otherwise normal settlement applies. |
| 3 | | *Are there limits on how many undos I can perform per day?* | Yes – a maximum of 3 undos per payer per day to mitigate abuse. |
| 4 | *How does the system prevent fraudsters from exploiting the undo window?* | Real‑time AML risk scoring, daily undo caps, and mandatory fund‑hold logs; any suspicious pattern triggers auto‑block. |
| 5 | *Do banks incur any additional fees for supporting the undo feature?* | No extra interchange; only a nominal processing fee for the reversible state (≈ 0.02 % of transaction value). |

**LLM Training Plan**  

1. **Data ingestion** – Pull the latest 12 months of UPI support tickets (≈ 1.2 M records) and the pilot‑phase chat logs (≈ 250 k).  
2. **Annotation** – Tag intents (undo‑request, fraud‑concern, settlement‑query) and map to the 5‑FAQ answers plus 30 deeper knowledge‑base articles.  
3. **Model** – Fine‑tune a domain‑specific LLM (GPT‑4‑Turbo) on the annotated corpus; enforce a **knowledge cutoff** at the latest RBI circular (Mar 2025).  
4. **Safety layers** – Implement a rule‑based guardrail that blocks any response suggesting a reversal beyond the 10‑second window.  
5. **Deployment** – Expose via NPCI’s “HelpBot” on the developer portal and partner support portals; monitor accuracy (target ≥ 92 % F1) and escalation rate (< 1 %).  

---

### Circular  
**Title** – *NPCI Circular No. UPI‑2026‑03: “Implementation of Ultra‑Short Undo Window for UPI Transactions”*  

| Element | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Scope** | All NPCI‑registered PSPs, banks, and third‑party apps that expose UPI APIs. |
| **Mandatory requirements** | 1. Support `undoFlag` in the `upiTxnRequest` payload. 2. Enforce ≤ 10 s grace period (configurable to 5 s for high‑risk accounts). 3. Implement real‑time risk‑scoring API (`/riskScore`). 4. Log reversible state with immutable audit trail (PCI‑DSS Level 1). 5. Provide daily undo‑cap reporting (max 3 per payer). |
| **Compliance deadline** | **30 June 2026** for API integration; **31 July 2026** for production rollout. |
| **Transition support** | NPCI will release a sandbox (April 2026), technical guide (PDF), and conduct three mandatory webinars (15 Apr, 12 May, 9 Jun). |
| **Penalties** | Non‑compliance after 31 July 2026 → de‑registration risk and possible RBI sanction per Payment Systems Act, 2007. |
| **Contact** | PMO – `upi‑undo@nPCI.org`; Technical Helpline – `1800‑425‑UPI`. |

---

### Product doc (w/ Specs, explanations, test cases/UI/UX guidelines)  
**Repository** – Confluence space `UPI‑Undo‑Feature` (access controlled).  

| Section | Owner | Core contents |
|---------|-------|----------------|
| **1. Overview & Vision** | Product Management | Business case, KPI targets, regulatory alignment. |
| **2. Functional Specs** | Architecture | API schema (`undoFlag`, `undoExpiry`), state‑machine diagram, error codes. |
| **3. Non‑Functional Specs** | Platform Engineering | Latency ≤ 0.5 s, throughput 3× current peak, availability 99.99 %. |
| **4. Security & Compliance** | Risk & Compliance | AML scoring model, PCI‑DSS controls, audit‑log format, liability matrix. |
| **5. UI/UX Guidelines** | Design | Mockups (undo banner, toast, disabled state), accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA), micro‑copy. |
| **6. Integration Guide** | Developer Relations | SDK snippets (Android/iOS, Web), sandbox onboarding steps, versioning policy. |
| **7. Test Suite** | QA | Unit tests (API validation), integration tests (end‑to‑end flow), performance scripts (JMeter), regression matrix, acceptance criteria (undo‑rate ≤ 2 s, fraud delta ≤ 0.5 %). |
| **8. Release & Ops Playbook** | Release Management | CI/CD pipeline, rollout phases, monitoring dashboards (Grafana), incident response run‑book. |
| **9. FAQ & Support** | Customer Support | Top 20 FAQs, escalation flow, LLM integration notes. |
| **10. Change Log** | Documentation Lead | Version history, author, review dates. |

**Maintenance** – Primary owner: **Product Manager – UPI Undo** (updates specs & KPI tracking).  
**Review cadence** – Quarterly technical review (Q1 2027 onward) and ad‑hoc updates for regulatory changes.  

---  

*All communications are aligned with NPCI branding guidelines and RBI’s “Consumer Protection in Digital Payments” framework.*

sections.title: 9. Potential Risks
sections.content:
## Potential Risks  

### **Fraud risk**  
- **Re‑use of the grace‑window for “payment‑reversal fraud”** – a fraudster initiates a high‑value payment, lets the merchant confirm receipt, then triggers the undo before settlement, effectively stealing the merchant’s goods/services.  
  - *Mitigation*:  
    - Real‑time risk‑scoring engine that blocks undo for transactions flagged as “high‑risk” (e.g., > ₹10 000, new beneficiary, velocity spikes).  
    - Auto‑escalation to “non‑reversible” mode for merchants flagged under RBI’s “Critical Service Provider” list.  
- **“Undo‑and‑re‑pay” laundering loop** – a criminal pays a victim, immediately undoes, then re‑pays using a different account to create a false “clean” transaction trail.  
  - *Mitigation*:  
    - Limit undo to **one per payer‑beneficiary pair per day** and enforce a **minimum settlement hold (≈ 30 s)** before the window opens.  
    - Log every undo request and feed to AML analytics (SAR generation after 3+ undos in 24 h).  
- **Denial‑of‑service via mass undos** – bots flood the API with undo calls, exhausting PSP processing capacity.  
  - *Mitigation*:  
    - Rate‑limit at **5 undo attempts per token per minute**; apply CAPTCHA for non‑human patterns.  
    - Deploy a “batch‑commit” model where undos are settled in a separate low‑priority queue, preserving core transaction throughput.  

### **Infosec risk**  
- **Extended transaction state exposure** – the payment remains in a “pending‑reversible” state, increasing the attack surface for man‑in‑the‑middle (MITM) or replay attacks.  
  - *Mitigation*:  
    - End‑to‑end encryption (TLS 1.3) with **transaction‑specific nonce** that expires after the undo window.  
    - Use **HMAC‑SHA256** signatures on undo requests; reject any request lacking a fresh nonce.  
- **Privilege escalation via undo API** – if authentication tokens are compromised, an attacker could reverse any recent payment.  
  - *Mitigation*:  
    - Enforce **multi‑factor authentication (MFA)** for undo actions on devices flagged as “high‑risk” (e.g., new device, IP change).  
    - Short‑lived OAuth access tokens (≤ 2 min) scoped only to “undo” operation.  
- **Logging leakage** – detailed undo logs (payer, beneficiary, amount, timestamps) could be harvested.  
  - *Mitigation*:  
    - Store logs in **encrypted audit tables** (AES‑256) with role‑based access control; retain only 90 days for operational use, then archive in masked form.  

### **Legal risk**  
- **Violation of RBI’s “finality of settlement” principle** – undoing a transaction after funds have been credited may be interpreted as retroactive settlement alteration.  
  - *Management*:  
    - Secure RBI sign‑off that the undo window **pre‑settlement** (i.e., before the inter‑bank settlement batch) is permissible; embed the rule in the UPI specification amendment.  
    - Limit undo to **≤ 2 seconds** of initiation, guaranteeing that the settlement engine has not yet debited the payer’s account.  
- **Merchant liability disputes** – merchants may claim loss for goods delivered before an undo.  
  - *Management*:  
    - Include a **standard clause** in the PSP‑merchant contract stating that any transaction undone within the grace period is **null and void**, and the merchant must not ship goods until settlement confirmation (≈ 2 s).  
    - Provide an **opt‑out** for “high‑value merchants” who can disable the undo feature for their accounts.  
- **Cross‑border compliance** – if a foreign PSP integrates the feature, differing consumer‑protection laws may apply.  
  - *Management*:  
    - Restrict the undo API to **domestic UPI IDs (VPA ending with .upi)**; block foreign‑originated VPAs.  

### **Data privacy risk**  
- **Processing of personal identifiers** – undo requests transmit payer’s mobile number, VPA, device ID, and location (for fraud scoring).  
  - *Controls*:  
    - Apply **privacy‑by‑design**: hash mobile numbers (SHA‑256) before storage; retain raw numbers only in encrypted vaults for 30 days.  
    - Conduct a **PII Impact Assessment** and register processing activities with the Data Protection Authority of India (DPDP Act).  
- **Behavioural profiling** – frequent undos could be used to infer user habits.  
  - *Controls*:  
    - Aggregate undo metrics at the **bank‑level** before analytics; no individual‑level profiling is retained beyond the 24‑hour operational window.  
    - Provide users an opt‑out from “undo‑based behavioural analytics” via the app settings.  

### **2nd order negative effect**  
- **Reduced post‑payment dispute vigilance** – users may become complacent, relying on the undo window and neglecting to verify merchant details before paying.  
  - *Mitigation*:  
    - Embed a **“double‑tap confirmation”** UI that highlights “You have 10 s to undo” and encourages a quick review.  
    - Run periodic **awareness campaigns** (in‑app push, SMS) on responsible payment practices.  
- **Liquidity pressure on PSPs** – holding funds in a reversible state for even 2 seconds across billions of transactions could marginally increase intra‑day float.  
  - *Mitigation*:  
    - Implement a **micro‑hold buffer** (≤ ₹0.01) that is released instantly on undo, ensuring negligible impact on PSP cash positions.  
    - Monitor daily “hold‑volume” metric; trigger auto‑scaling of settlement nodes if hold‑volume exceeds **0.05 % of total transaction value**.  
- **Erosion of merchant confidence** – merchants may perceive the feature as a risk to cash flow, potentially delaying shipments.  
  - *Mitigation*:  
    - Offer an **“merchant‑protected” mode** where undos are disabled for transactions above a configurable threshold (e.g., ₹5 000).  
    - Publish real‑time settlement confirmation APIs so merchants can verify finality before dispatch.  

---  

*All risks and mitigations are aligned with RBI’s “Payment System Oversight” guidelines (2024), NPCI’s security standards (PCI‑DSS, ISO 27001), and the DPDP Act (2023). Continuous monitoring and quarterly risk‑review cycles will ensure compliance and operational resilience.*

sections.title: 10. Compliance
sections.content:
**Existing guideline change** *Which existing RBI/NPCI guidelines need to be amended? What changes are required?* | Guideline (RBI / NPCI) | Current wording that blocks an ultra‑short undo | Required amendment (concise) | |------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|------------------------------| | **RBI Master Direction – “Finality of Settlement” (Mar 2025)** | “A UPI transaction, once authorised, is deemed final and irrevocable.” | Insert a **“Grace‑Period Clause”**: *“For transactions flagged with the `UNDO‑FLAG`, finality is deferred for a maximum of 15 seconds post‑authorisation, during which the funds remain in a **hold state** and may be reversed without settlement.”* | | **RBI Circular on “Consumer Protection in Digital Payments” (Oct 2024)** | No provision for pre‑settlement cancellation. | Add a **“Consumer Undo Right”** sub‑section mandating that PSPs expose an ultra‑short undo option (≤ 15 s) for all retail UPI payments, subject to risk‑score thresholds. | | **NPCI UPI Specification – “Transaction Lifecycle” (v2.3, Sep 2024)** | “Step 5 – Settlement is instantaneous.” | Revise Step 5 to **“Conditional Settlement”**: *If `UNDO‑FLAG` is active and no reversal request is received within the grace window, the transaction proceeds to settlement; otherwise, it is aborted and the hold released.* | | **RBI Circular on “Risk Management Framework for UPI” (Jan 2025)** | “Reversal mechanisms are limited to post‑settlement charge‑backs.” | Expand to include **“Real‑time Reversal (pre‑settlement)”** with mandatory real‑time risk‑scoring (≤ 200 ms) and fraud‑mitigation controls (velocity, device fingerprint, AML watch‑list). | | **PCI‑DSS Annex – “Tokenisation for UPI” (2024)** | No mention of temporary token hold. | Amend to require that the **tokenised PAN** remain in a **non‑settlement hold** state during the undo window, with audit logs for every hold/release event. | --- **New guideline addition** *What new guidelines or circulars need to be issued? What should they cover?* | New Circular / Guideline (Proposed Title) | Core Coverage (bullet points) | |--------------------------------------------|--------------------------------| | **RBI Circular “Ultra‑Short Undo Window for UPI Transactions” (2026‑01)** | • Scope: All retail‑class UPI transactions ≤ ₹2 Lakhs.
• Grace‑Period: 10‑15 seconds configurable by PSPs within the limit.
• **Eligibility Rules** – only transactions with risk‑score < 80 (out of 100) qualify; high‑risk (e.g., > ₹1 Lakhs, new device) are excluded.
• **Liability Matrix** – PSP that initiates the hold bears reversal liability; merchant bears charge‑back liability only after the window lapses.
• **Consumer Consent** – mandatory UI prompt “Tap ‘Undo’ within 15 s to cancel”.
• **Audit & Reporting** – PSPs must submit daily logs of all hold/release events to NPCI (CSV format, encrypted).
• **SLAs** – reversal processing ≤ 2 seconds; hold release ≤ 1 second.
• **Penalties** – non‑compliance > 0.5 % of total UPI volume → ₹5 crore fine per quarter. | | **NPCI Product Circular “UPI Transaction Hold & Reversal API Specification” (v3.0)** | • New API flag `undo_flag` (boolean) and `undo_expiry` (timestamp).
• **Hold‑State Response Codes** – `H01` (hold created), `H02` (undo accepted), `H03` (hold expired).
• **Real‑time Risk‑Scoring Interface** – mandatory call to `riskScoreService` (≤ 200 ms).
• **Security** – end‑to‑end encryption, token‑masked PAN, PCI‑DSS Level 1 compliance for hold data.
• **Rollback Logic** – atomic DB transaction ensuring no double‑spend; idempotent `undo` call.
• **Monitoring** – mandatory metrics: `undo_success_rate`, `undo_latency`, `hold_duration_avg`. | | **RBI Circular “Enhanced AML Controls for Pre‑Settlement Holds”** | • Mandatory AML screening of the **hold‑state** transaction (watch‑list, SAR generation).
• **KYC Refresh** – if hold exceeds 12 seconds (edge case), trigger secondary KYC verification.
• **Reporting** – any reversal flagged as “potential fraud” must be reported to FIU within 24 hours. | --- **Must have compliances in NPCI product circular for ecosystem** *List the mandatory compliance requirements that all ecosystem participants must meet as specified in the NPCI circular* 1. **Grace‑Period Implementation** - All PSPs must support a **10‑15 second hold** on funds for transactions flagged with `undo_flag`. - Hold must be released automatically if no undo request is received within the window. 2. **Real‑time Risk Scoring** - Integrate the **NPCI‑mandated risk‑score service**; score ≤ 80 required for eligibility. - Scoring latency ≤ 200 ms per transaction; failure to meet latency → transaction must proceed without undo capability. 3. **Liability & Reversal Logic** - PSP that initiates the hold bears **full reversal liability** for any undo request within the window. - Merchant liability only applies post‑window (standard charge‑back rules). 4. **Consumer Consent UI** - Mandatory on‑screen prompt: “You have **X seconds** to undo this payment.” - Prompt must be **visible for the entire grace period** and must not be dismissible before expiry. 5. **Audit Trail & Reporting** - Log every hold creation, undo request, and hold release with: transaction ID, timestamps, risk score, user ID, PSP ID. - Submit **daily encrypted logs** to NPCI via the secure SFTP gateway (file format: JSON‑L). 6. **Security & Data Protection** - End‑to‑end encryption (TLS 1.3) for all API traffic. - Tokenised PAN must remain masked; hold data must be stored in **PCI‑DSS Level 1** compliant vaults. 7. **Performance SLAs** - **Undo‑Latency** ≤ 2 seconds from user tap to reversal confirmation. - **Hold‑Release Latency** ≤ 1 second after window expiry. - System availability for undo service ≥ 99.9 % (monthly). 8. **AML/KYC Controls** - Perform AML screening on the **hold‑state transaction**; any match → auto‑block and flag to FIU. - If hold exceeds 12 seconds (exceptional case), trigger secondary KYC verification. 9. **Monitoring & Metrics** - Publish the following metrics to NPCI dashboards:
• `undo_success_rate` (target ≥ 98 %)
• `undo_latency_avg` (target ≤ 1.5 s)
• `hold_duration_avg` (target ≤ 13 s)
• `fraud_incident_delta` (≤ 0.5 % of undo volume). 10. **Penalty & Remediation** - Exceeding any SLA or compliance threshold for **> 0.5 %** of total UPI volume triggers a **₹5 crore per quarter** penalty, plus mandatory remediation plan submission within 30 days. These mandatory items form the backbone of the NPCI product circular for the ultra‑short “undo” window, ensuring the feature is **secure, consumer‑friendly, regulatorily compliant, and scalable** across the Indian payments ecosystem.
